title: seedExamples short-circuit blocks new substrate files from reaching already-seeded projects
body:
Per memory feedback_seed_examples_does_not_overwrite, seedExamples() short-circuits on existing seeded files — bundled examples updates do not propagate to seeded .pi/monitors/ in already-initialized projects. Same pattern blocks substrate-arc landings: when production lands config.json + relations.json + new schemas (config.schema.json, relations.schema.json), already-seeded projects don't get them. Two paths: (a) seedExamples gains an additive-only path that copies missing files without overwriting existing user customizations; (b) /project init gains a --migrate flag that diffs against bundled defaults and seeds missing pieces. Without one of these, every existing user must hand-copy new schemas + bootstrap config. Surfaced as gap #31 in the integration-map review.

---

DEC-0011 (enacted 2026-05-03) supersedes this issue's framing. The decision is to ship no packaged defaults; users install desired schemas and starter blocks via an opt-in mechanism. The seedExamples / additive-only / migration question is moot at the source: the artifacts that would need to be seeded or migrated are no longer shipped. This issue is reframed as 'specify and implement the opt-in install mechanism that replaces the default-copying loops at packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:215-236'. Implementation work proceeds under DEC-0011's direction, not the original seedExamples-fix framing.
